claim: Tie rule differs (> vs Polymarket >=) but every SETTLE path shields it behind a defer-to-oracle margin band; only the unguarded live-leader surface is exposed.

epoch_alignment.note: candles are UNIX seconds; join exact; no off-by-one/timezone issue

code_facts.verdictOf_line810: return {up:d>0} but only reached when |d|>=clearMargin (line809 defers |d|<~$19)
code_facts.provisional_settle_line1645: apply_settle('up' if gap>0 else 'down') gated by clear(|gap|>=max($15,0.03%)) OR (age>180 and |gap|>=2); gap==0 can never fire
code_facts.leader_feed_proxy_line928: up = pv['ret'] >= 0  -> uses CORRECT >= tie convention

correction: Claim structurally correct but under-attributes the exposure to the TIE rule. The >/>= tie convention never triggered (0/863). The real near-boundary error on the unguarded live-leader surface is the COINBASE-vs-CHAINLINK reference divergence: 2.20% overall, 9.45% inside the defer band, ~27% for sub-1bps moves. The tie is the measure-zero center of that divergence. All booking paths remain fully shielded.
